body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Scenario

Primitive exercised: Interrupt-during-turn. The canonical user-observable signal is `ready ‚Üí working ‚Üí ready` ‚Äî the session must not stick in `working` after the cancel, and the next prompt must drive a fresh `ready ‚Üí working ‚Üí ready` arc. The scenario's interrupt key for aider is **Ctrl-C**, not ESC (per scenario-meanings; aider has no ESC-to-interrupt binding). The matched capability anchor is `headless_mode` (the scenario's declared `requires`), which is `true` in aider's capabilities.json ‚Äî there is no dedicated interrupt capability key.

## agent_supports: yes

Aider supports cancelling an in-flight turn with Ctrl-C. `Coder.keyboard_interrupt()` (aider/coders/base_coder.py:986) catches the first SIGINT and warns `^C again to exit`; the in-flight LLM stream is aborted via the `except KeyboardInterrupt` handler in `Coder.send()` (base_coder.py:1819). The interrupted-turn bookkeeping appends `^C KeyboardInterrupt` to the current user message and an assistant note `"I see that you interrupted my previous reply."` (base_coder.py:1575-1582). A second Ctrl-C within 2s exits the process; a single Ctrl-C returns cleanly to the prompt, ready for the next turn. This is confirmed in source for the installed aider 0.86.2.

## daemon_capability: full

The daemon DOES deliver the canonical user-observable signal ‚Äî `ready ‚Üí working ‚Üí ready`, no sticking in `working` ‚Äî which is the entire aider-applicable spec for this scenario. It just does so through the GENERIC turn-end path, not interrupt-aware machinery.

What lands in `.aider.chat.history.md` on a Ctrl-C interrupt:
1. `#### <prompt>` ‚Äî the user prompt (parser emits `user_message`, opens the turn ‚Üí `working`).
2. Partial assistant prose ‚Äî `Coder.send()`'s `finally` block runs `io.ai_output(self.partial_response_content)` (base_coder.py:1828), flushing whatever streamed before the cancel as plain markdown lines (the parser buffers these).
3. `> ^C again to exit` ‚Äî a blockquote (`tool_warning` ‚Üí `_tool_message` ‚Üí `append_chat_history(blockquote=True)`, io.py:992). The parser treats it as ordinary aider status output and ignores it.

Crucially there is **no `> Tokens:` line** (the turn aborted before any model call reported usage) and **no error blockquote** (`^C` markers do not match the parser's `errorRE`, verified empirically). So the parser's two explicit turn-closers never fire. The turn stays open until the transcript goes quiet for 1500ms, at which point `IdleFlush` synthesizes a payload-free `turn_done` (parser.go:216). That flips `LastEventType`, and the state classifier returns the session to `ready` via **rule 2** ("agent finished turn ‚Üí ready", state_classifier.go:58) ‚Äî the same generic idle-completion path every aider turn takes. The recipe's verify steps confirm exactly this: a `state_transition‚Üíready` on the same rollout session within the IdleFlush + 5s staleWorking window, never sticking in `working`, never entering `waiting`.

This is `full`, not `bug`: the daemon mis-handles nothing. A trace exists in the watched Source (FilesUnderCWD `.aider.chat.history.md`) and the parser handles it correctly for the spec's user-observable assertion. The ONLY thing irrlicht cannot do is recognize the turn AS an interrupt ‚Äî but interrupt IDENTITY is not part of the aider-applicable spec. The interrupt-specific transport the scenario's `verify.contains_interrupt` and the spec's interrupt-marker assertion describe ‚Äî `recordUserInterruptFlags` / `IsUserInterrupt` / sticky `LastWasUserInterrupt` / classifier rule 3 with reason "user ESC interrupt" ‚Äî is entirely claudecode-specific (claudecode/parser.go:265, state_classifier.go:73), keyed on claudecode's literal `[Request interrupted by user]` JSONL marker. aider's markdown emits no such marker, so that machinery never engages. Per the #476 authoring rule, a narrow gap the canonical (aider-applicable) spec does NOT require is a **caveat**, not a downgrade ‚Äî so `daemon_capability` stays `full` and the interrupt-invisibility lives in `caveats`. This tracks the sibling `interrupted-turn` cell (assessed full): same generic IdleFlush turn-end, identical transport.

## driver_capability: ready

The interactive driver (`scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh`) implements every step the recipe needs: `send` (the long-essay prompt), `sleep` (let the model start streaming, then settle), and `interrupt` ‚Äî `step_interrupt()` (line 183-185) sends `C-c` to the tmux pane, which IS aider's cancel signal (aider has no separate ESC binding; Ctrl-C is the cancel). No `wait_turn` follows the interrupt by design (the cancelled turn never completes a model call). No gapped primitive is exercised ‚Äî no `keys`/`sigkill`/`restart`/`session`. Hence `ready`.

## Matrix

The coverage cell already carries the two-axis shape (`daemon_capability: unknown`, `driver_capability: ready`). This resolves the `unknown` to `full`. The maintainer should update the daemon axis to `full` and refresh the note to reflect that the ready‚Üíworking‚Üíready arc is observed via the generic IdleFlush path (interrupt identity is not, but is not spec-required for aider).
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: irrlicht cannot tell an interrupted turn apart from a normally-completed one. The interrupt keystroke itself is invisible to the parser; resolution to ready is the generic IdleFlush turn_done + classifier rule 2 ("agent finished turn ‚Üí ready"), not the interrupt-specific rule 3 ("user ESC interrupt"). aider's markdown carries no [Request interrupted by user]-style marker, so IsUserInterrupt/LastWasUserInterrupt never set. This is a non-spec gap (the aider-applicable spec is the user-observable ready‚Üíworking‚Üíready arc), hence a caveat rather than a daemon bug.

The scenario's verify.contains_interrupt and the claudecode-shaped spec assertion (a user line beginning '[Request interrupted by user]') cannot be satisfied by an aider transcript; do NOT assert an interrupt marker or a rule-3 transition for aider. The aider-applicable assertion is the user-observable ready‚Üíworking‚Üíready arc plus a clean fresh arc on the next prompt.

Working‚Üíready after the cancel is gated on the 1500ms idle window AND the daemon's 5s staleWorkingRefreshInterval, so the user-visible return to ready can lag the Ctrl-C by up to ~5s ‚Äî same latency as any clean aider turn end, not specific to interrupts.

sources.note: aider markdown parser: no interrupt-marker handling; turn closes only via > Tokens: (closeModelCall), error blockquote (flushErrorTurn), or IdleFlush (1500ms) ‚Äî Ctrl-C hits none of the first two, so the generic IdleFlush turn_done synthesizes the working‚Üíready the spec requires

rule 3 (reason 'user ESC interrupt') fires only on LastWasUserInterrupt/LastWasToolDenial; aider's idle-flush turn_done resolves via rule 2 'agent finished turn ‚Üí ready' instead ‚Äî the daemon delivers ready‚Üíworking‚Üíready correctly, just not via interrupt-aware machinery

recordUserInterruptFlags / IsUserInterrupt ‚Äî the interrupt-detection machinery is claudecode-specific, keyed on the literal '[Request interrupted by user]' JSONL text marker; aider has no analog, which is why interrupt identity (not spec-required for aider) is a caveat not a bug

step_interrupt() sends tmux C-c ‚Äî the driver implements the recipe's interrupt step (plus send/sleep); aider's cancel is Ctrl-C, no separate ESC binding, so driver_capability is ready with no gapped primitive

aider 0.86.2 Coder.keyboard_interrupt() ‚Äî single Ctrl-C warns '^C again to exit', double Ctrl-C exits; send() finally flushes partial_response_content via io.ai_output (line 1828); interrupted turn appends '^C KeyboardInterrupt' to cur_messages (lines 1575-1582)

tool_warning ('^C again to exit') routes through _tool_message ‚Üí append_chat_history(blockquote=True), so the Ctrl-C warning IS written to .aider.chat.history.md as a '> ^C ...' blockquote that the irrlicht parser ignores

Primitive exercised names Ctrl-C as aider's cancel signal (ESC for claudecode/codex/pi); user-observable signal is ready‚Üíworking‚Üíready with no sticky working ‚Äî interrupt identity is not part of the aider-applicable spec
